name: PriceScout
audience: Thrift store owners and staff, estate sale operators, yard sale runners, flea market dealers, and the resellers who feed them. B2B-leaning — designed for crews of 2-5 people scanning donation piles or sale tables together.
value_prop: Run a thrift store, estate sale, or yard sale? Stop guessing what donations are worth. Point a phone at any item, get the resale price in seconds. Up to 4 phones per subscription so your whole crew scans at once.

device_policy.note: Every paid tier includes 4 active scanner installs (Android first, iOS later). Stores with bigger crews add scanners at $15/mo each. License keys bind to install-id; operator can rotate one out and add another anytime via the app's Devices tab.

pricing_tiers.name: Week Pass
features: Perfect for one-weekend estate or yard sales

pricing_tiers.name: Pro Monthly
features: Shared flip log across the crew

pricing_tiers.name: Pro Annual
features: Best for thrift stores running year-round

pricing_tiers.name: Founders Lifetime
features: Up to 4 scanner installs included, forever

pricing_notes: Repositioned 2026-04-29 around thrift stores / estate sales / yard sales as primary buyer (multi-staff crews), with individual resellers secondary. Four tiers: Week Pass $29/wk, Pro Monthly $49/mo, Pro Annual $490/yr (~17% saving), Founders Lifetime $699 once (cap 100). Every tier includes 4 scanner installs; additional scanners are +$15/mo each metered separately via STRIPE_PRICE_DEVICE_ADDON_MONTHLY. Stripe Price IDs are immutable — any future tier change creates new IDs, old ones archived after env swap.
vision_notes: Primary: Claude Haiku (cheap) for first-pass identify; escalate to Claude Sonnet for ambiguous items. Alternate: OpenRouter (Gemini Flash for cost). Image is sent server-side; never store raw images by default — ephemeral lookup only.
comp_options_to_evaluate: eBay Browse API (sold-listings via /buy/marketplace_insights/v1_beta/item_sales — limited, US-only, requires application)
eBay Finding API (active listings only, no sold; use as price-floor proxy)
Keepa (Amazon-only, but covers a huge chunk of branded thrift inventory)
ScrapingBee/Apify managed scraper (paid, ToS-shifting onto vendor)
